title: heliocereus schrankii subsp. helenae
intro: Heliocereus schrankii subsp. helenae, also known as Helen's Night-Blooming Cereus, is a rare and unique species of cactus native to Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, inflammation, and various types of infections, due to its high content of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Heliocereus schrankii subsp. helenae include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain and inflammation, and even exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
